annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":58000,"title":"[SQL] Docs: README missing inline snippets, deprecated API migration guidance, and SQL-specific key concepts for 1.4.0","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` **Service directory:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57982) by `@ArthurMa1978` ## What Changed Released `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` 1.4.0 targeting API version 2025-01-01. The release includes: - Upgraded API version from `package-2023-08` to `2025-01-01` - `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` marked `[Obsolete]` in favor of `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` - Methods dependent on the retiring 2014-04-01 API version marked obsolete (introduced in 1.4.0-beta.2) - Library declared AOT-compatible (introduced in 1.4.0-beta.2) ## Gaps Found - `tests/Samples/ReadMe.cs` defines snippet regions (`Snippet:Readme_DefaultSubscription`, `Snippet:Readme_GetResourceGroupCollection`) but the README.md contains no `C# Snippet:` placeholder blocks to inject them — so the snippets are silently ignored - The `Examples` section of the README contains no inline code examples; it only links to an external page - The `Key concepts` section is an empty boilerplate link with no SQL-specific content (no mention of SqlServer, SqlDatabase, ManagedInstance, ElasticPool, or the resource hierarchy) - No deprecation/migration notice for `SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` → `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` in `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability` - No mention anywhere in the README of the 2014-04-01 API retirement or guidance to migrate off the obsolete methods - No mention of the AOT-compatibility support added in 1.4.0 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` (edit) Replace the current minimal `Key concepts` section with SQL-specific content covering the main resource types: ```markdown ## Key concepts `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` organizes SQL resources in a hierarchy that mirrors the Azure resource model: - **SqlServerResource** – Represents a logical SQL server (host for databases, firewall rules, etc.) - **SqlDatabaseResource** – Represents a single database within a SQL server - **ElasticPoolResource** – Represents an elastic pool grouping databases that share resources - **ManagedInstanceResource** – Represents a SQL Managed Instance (PaaS with full SQL Server compatibility) - **ManagedDatabaseResource** – Represents a database hosted on a SQL Managed Instance Each resource class follows the Azure SDK pattern: - A `*Collection` class for listing and creating resources - A `*Resource` class for reading, updating, and deleting an individual resource - Extension methods on `ResourceGroupResource`, `SubscriptionResource`, and `ArmClient` to obtain collection objects > ⚠️ **Deprecation notice (1.4.0):** `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` has been marked obsolete. Use `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` instead. > > Several methods that relied on the retiring Azure SQL 2014-04-01 API version have also been marked obsolete. See the [Azure SQL REST API retirement notice]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for migration guidance. ``` Replace the current `Examples` section with snippet placeholders and sample links: ```markdown ## Examples ### Authenticate and get the default subscription ``` ``` ### Create a resource group ``` ``` For complete sample walkthroughs, see: - [Managing Managed Instances](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.md) - [Managing Managed Databases](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.md) ``` Add an AOT note to the `Getting started` section (after the `Authenticate the Client` paragraph): ```markdown ### AOT compatibility `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` 1.4.0 and later supports [Native AOT]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) compilation. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippets in `tests/Samples/ReadMe.cs` are already defined correctly: - `Snippet:Readme_DefaultSubscription` — creates an `ArmClient` and retrieves the default subscription - `Snippet:Readme_GetResourceGroupCollection` — creates a resource group No changes are needed to the test file. The snippet placeholders added in Step 1 will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1`. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently has all six required sections, but their content needs the improvements above: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | | Key concepts | ❌ Empty boilerplate | | Examples | ❌ No inline code, no snippets | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ✅ Present | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for tone and formatting. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 sqlmanagement` — confirm README is updated with snippet content in the git diff 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 sqlmanagement` — no public API was changed by this doc update, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24187153008/agentic_workflow) · ● 594.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58000","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T11:20:21Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T11:20:21Z"}

The release includes: - Upgraded API version from `package-2023-08` to `2025-01-01` - `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` marked `[Obsolete]` in favor of `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` - Methods dependent on the retiring 2014-04-01 API version marked obsolete (introduced in 1.4.0-beta.2) - Library declared AOT-compatible (introduced in 1.4.0-beta.2) ## Gaps Found - `tests/Samples/ReadMe.cs` defines snippet regions (`Snippet:Readme_DefaultSubscription`, `Snippet:Readme_GetResourceGroupCollection`) but the README.md contains no `C# Snippet:` placeholder blocks to inject them — so the snippets are silently ignored - The `Examples` section of the README contains no inline code examples; Use `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` instead. > > Several methods that relied on the retiring Azure SQL 2014-04-01 API version have also been marked obsolete. See the [Azure SQL REST API retirement notice]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for migration guidance. ``` Replace the current `Examples` section with snippet placeholders and sample links: ```markdown ## Examples ### Authenticate and get the default subscription ``` ``` ### Create a resource group ``` ``` For complete sample walkthroughs, see: - [Managing Managed Instances](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.md) - [Managing Managed Databases](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.md) ``` Add an AOT note to the `Getting started` section (after the `Authenticate the Client` paragraph): ```markdown ### AOT compatibility `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` 1.4.0 and later supports [Native AOT]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) compilation. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippets in `tests/Samples/ReadMe.cs` are already defined correctly: - `Snippet:Readme_DefaultSubscription` — creates an `ArmClient` and retrieves the default subscription - `Snippet:Readme_GetResourceGroupCollection` — creates a resource group No changes are needed to the test file. The snippet placeholders added in Step 1 will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1`. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently has all six required sections, but their content needs the improvements above: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | | Key concepts | ❌ Empty boilerplate | | Examples | ❌ No inline code, no snippets | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ✅ Present | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for tone and formatting. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 sqlmanagement` — confirm README is updated with snippet content in the git diff 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 sqlmanagement` — no public API was changed by this doc update, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24187153008/agentic_workflow) · ● 594.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58000","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T11:20:21Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T11:20:21Z"}

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` 1.4.0 — specifically missing inline code snippets, no deprecation/migration guidance for obsolete APIs, empty key-concepts section, and no mention of AOT compatibility.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` 1.4.0
**Affected API:** `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` (obsolete), 2014-04-01 API methods (obsolete), README.md
**Scenarios:**
- `tests/Samples/ReadMe.cs` defines snippet regions but README.md has no `C# Snippet:` placeholders to inject them
- `Examples` section has no inline code, only an external link
- `Key concepts` section is empty boilerplate with no SQL-specific content
- No deprecation/migration notice for `SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` → `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB`
- No mention of 2014-04-01 API retirement or obsolete method migration guidance
- No mention of AOT compatibility introduced in 1.4.0

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` with SQL-specific key concepts, inline snippet placeholders, deprecation notices, and AOT note.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This issue was automatically generated by the "Update Docs" workflow triggered by commit [REDACTED] (PR #57982). The gaps are documentation-only; no runtime behavior is affected.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` and `tests/Samples/ReadMe.cs` to verify the snippet region names align with what the README needs.
2. Add `C# Snippet:` placeholder blocks to README.md for `Snippet:Readme_DefaultSubscription` and `Snippet:Readme_GetResourceGroupCollection`.
3. Fill in the `Key concepts` section with SQL resource hierarchy content (SqlServer, SqlDatabase, ElasticPool, ManagedInstance, ManagedDatabase).
4. Add deprecation notices for `SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` → `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` and for 2014-04-01 API methods.
5. Add an AOT compatibility note to the `Getting started` section.
6. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 sqlmanagement` and verify README gets updated in the git diff.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — The package `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` is a management-plane SDK (Azure.ResourceManager.* prefix), matching the Mgmt category definition.
- **Service:** `SQL` — The issue explicitly references `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` and the `sdk/sqlmanagement/` directory. Reference issues #45727, #44763, and #42027 confirm `SQL` is the correct service label for this package.
- **Confidence:** High — Both labels are unambiguously identified from the issue content and corroborated by multiple quality reference issues.
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %SQL %Mgmt` (line 1146) — Both predicted labels `SQL` and `Mgmt` are present in the issue; this is the first (bottom-to-top) entry that matches all required labels.
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ericshape
- **Routing action:** Added `Service Attention` label; issue left unassigned (ServiceOwners path, no AzureSdkOwners). No `needs-team-attention` label added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported).
- **Scan notes:** Entries for `%SQL - VM %Mgmt` (1164), `%SQL - Replication & Failover %Mgmt` (1161), `%SQL - Managed Instance %Mgmt` (1158), `%SQL - Elastic Jobs %Mgmt` (1155), `%SQL - Data Security %Mgmt` (1152), and `%SQL - Backup & Restore %Mgmt` (1149) were all encountered first during bottom-to-top scan but skipped because they require a more specific SQL sub-service label not present in the issue.
</details>
